raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/2025, I placed an order for two items XXXX XXXX -- which were gifts ; Order # : XXXX. One item cost {$67.00} and the other {$190.00}. Upon receipt of the order around XX/XX/XXXX, one of the two items was the wrong size. I contacted Macy 's for a resolution. I was told that I could return the XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX. After speaking with Macy 's corporate office ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ) and chatting with a rep, I returned the XXXX  for a refund. I returned the item with the label that was provided by Macy 's and received an email that they received and credited me on XX/XX/2025 ; return completed date XX/XX/2025. I returned the item, but the credit wasnt posted to my account. 

When I received my statement with a payment due date of XX/XX/2025, NO PAYMENT WAS DUE. I made an online payment of {$67.00} on XX/XX/2025 to pay for the one item I ordered which came in the right size ; I kept. 

I received the statement with a payment due date of XX/XX/2025 and it reflected a balance of {$260.00}. I considered it may take time for Macy 's and their credit card Citibank to catch up with the return. 

When I checked the online account around XX/XX/XXXX to make sure I received the credit for the {$190.00} XXXX I returned. However, in checking the statement with a payment due date of XX/XX/2025 it reflected my payment of {$200.00} was past due. I then attempted to dispute the bill online on but Macy's.com system does not have that option available on line TO DISPUTE THE BILL. The only option is dispute a charge. 

Citibank Macys Account Statement due XX/XX/2025 reflects my payment as past due coupled with a {$30.00} late fee plus interest. when it should be {$0.00} with NO PAYMENT DUE. The most recent statement with a payment due date of XX/XX/2025 Payment Information is a New Balance {$240.00} with a minimum payment due of {$68.00} -- whereas the statement should reflect NO PAYMENT DUE. 

I do not owe any money to Macy 's Credit Card/Citibank ..
